sections.title: Deep Pattern: Capability-Centric Architecture for Multi-Agent Systems
content: What it solves: Most multi-agent systems hardcode dependencies between orchestrators and workers. Swap a model backend → everything downstream breaks. CCA treats each agent capability as an atomic unit with formal contracts.

How it works: Capability Registry acts as the coordination hub. Lifecycle Manager handles startup/shutdown via dependency graphs. Evolution Envelopes version capabilities without breaking running systems.
Production example: 6-agent software engineering pipeline (Requirements → Code Gen → Test Gen → Review → Docs → PR), each on different backends (Claude, GPT-5.3, Gemini).

Your relevance: Seedify's Notion ingestion, PKM indexing, and Odoo task creation are different capabilities with different reliability requirements. CCA's contracts let you swap Notion for Confluence without refactoring the orchestrator.
